name: Local Video Draft Machine
positioning: A local-first video operations system that proves draft-to-MP4 quality before publishing integrations go live.

audience.primary: Solo founders, creator-operators, and product teams building repeatable short-form video workflows.
audience.secondary: Technical marketers and automation-minded content leads who need local proof before distribution.
needs: Trustworthy local artifacts before platform uploads
Clear provenance across drafts, renders, approvals, and publishing payloads
Practical content packages that can be reviewed without source-code spelunking

do: Use direct claims grounded in generated artifacts.
Name the workflow stage and next operator action.
avoid: Overpromising live publishing before credentials and approval gates exist.
Generic creator-economy language without operational detail.
Claims that imply third-party platform submission in dry-run mode.

motion.sceneRhythm: fast hook resolve, steady explain frame, structured proof card, calm final slate

typography.title: Bold, high-contrast title treatment sized for vertical mobile viewing.
typography.body: Large concise support copy with no more than two lines per scene.
typography.caption: Sidecar SRT plus visible in-video text for accessibility and review.

assets.notes: Placeholders describe intended brand asset slots only; no external asset lookup or generation is performed.

disclosures: Generated artifacts are local dry-run outputs until human approval and platform credentials are added.
Provider and publish payloads must retain provenance back to draft packages and local renders.
AI-generated or assisted content should be disclosed where platform policy or user context requires it.
blockedClaims: Do not claim videos were uploaded or published by the system in dry-run mode.
Do not imply platform endorsement or affiliation.
Do not remove human review requirements from public-distribution copy.

derivedFrom: current local render and publish-prep workflow assumptions
